opening_void_beast.text: Arrel stood over the stain it left behind, sword still raised, though there was nothing left to cut.
opening_void_beast.text_ko: 아렐은 그것이 남긴 얼룩 앞에 서 있었다. 검은 아직 치켜든 채였지만, 더 이상 벨 것은 없었다.

opening_void_beast.text: His skull throbbed. Not the clean pain of a headache but a deeper pressure, as if someone had scooped out a piece of his brain and left the cavity unsealed.
opening_void_beast.text_ko: 두개골이 욱신거렸다. 깨끗한 두통이 아니라, 뇌의 한 조각을 도려낸 뒤 그 빈자리를 봉합하지 않은 듯한 깊은 압박이었다.

opening_void_beast.speaker: system_log
opening_void_beast.text:
[COMBUSTION DETECTED: Type 1 — Sensory Fragment]
[LOCATION EST.: Rim Exterior, Achen Forest]
[RESIDUAL RECORD: trace detected — fading]
opening_void_beast.text_ko:
[연소 감지: 유형 1 — 감각 파편]
[위치 추정: 림 외곽, 아켄 숲]
[잔류 기록: 흔적 감지 — 소실 중]

elia_appears.text: Her voice, close. He hadn't heard her approach.
elia_appears.text_ko: 가까이에서 들려온 그녀의 목소리. 그는 그녀가 다가오는 소리를 듣지 못했다.

elia_appears.text: His right hand twitched. It moved toward her before he could stop it, fingers uncurling from the sword grip, reaching for something his muscles remembered but his mind did not.
elia_appears.text_ko: 오른손이 움찔했다. 멈추기도 전에 그녀를 향해 움직였고, 검 손잡이를 쥔 손가락이 풀리며 머리는 잊었지만 근육은 기억하는 무언가를 향해 뻗었다.

elia_appears.speaker: Arrel
elia_appears.text: One burn. Grade three, maybe. Nothing I'll miss.
elia_appears.text_ko: 한 번 태웠어. 3등급쯤. 없어져도 아깝지 않은 거야.

elia_appears.text: That was probably true. That was probably a lie.
The problem with burning memories was that you could never be sure which it was.
elia_appears.text_ko: 기억을 태우는 일의 문제는, 어느 쪽인지 절대 확신할 수 없다는 데 있었다.

ash_rain.text: A gray flake, lighter than dust, dissolving against his skin with a faint warmth that was not his own.
ash_rain.text_ko: 먼지보다 가벼운 회색 조각 하나가 피부 위에서 녹아들었다. 희미한 온기가 있었지만, 그의 것은 아니었다.

ash_rain.text: It came down through the canopy in slow spirals, each flake carrying the residue of someone else's loss.
ash_rain.text_ko: 재는 나무 사이로 느린 나선처럼 떨어졌다. 조각마다 누군가가 잃어버린 것의 잔재를 품고 있었다.

ash_rain.text: He brushed the dissolving flake from his cheek. Someone else's sorrow. Not his. Not anymore.
ash_rain.text_ko: 그는 녹아드는 조각을 뺨에서 털어냈다. 다른 누군가의 슬픔. 그의 것이 아니었다. 이제는.

ash_rain.speaker: Elia
ash_rain.text: We should move. The rain will get thicker after dark.
ash_rain.text_ko: 움직여야 해. 해가 지면 비가 더 짙어질 거야.

camp_night.text: No fire. Fire drew attention, and attention out here meant things with too few memories and too many teeth.
camp_night.text_ko: 불은 피우지 않았다. 불은 시선을 끌고, 이곳에서 시선은 기억은 너무 적고 이빨은 너무 많은 것들을 뜻했다.

camp_night.text: He pressed it flat against his knee and held it there until the tremors became indistinguishable from his own heartbeat.
camp_night.text_ko: 그는 손을 무릎에 눌러 붙이고, 떨림이 자신의 심장 박동과 구분되지 않을 때까지 버텼다.

choices.text: Try to remember what you burned.

camp_night.text: Elia hummed low, a melody with no beginning and no end. It filled the space behind his eyes and made the gaps feel less sharp.
camp_night.text_ko: 엘리아는 낮게 흥얼거렸다. 시작도 끝도 없는 선율이었다. 그 노래는 그의 눈 뒤 빈자리를 채우고, 공허의 가장자리를 조금 무디게 만들었다.

camp_night.text: Morning would come. The Belt waited ahead. And somewhere beyond it, the answers he was trading himself to find.
camp_night.text_ko: 아침은 올 것이다. 벨트가 앞에 기다리고 있었다. 그리고 그 너머 어딘가에, 그가 자기 자신과 맞바꾸며 찾는 답이 있었다.

elia_talk.speaker: Elia
elia_talk.text: Stay close. The Rim gets worse at night.
elia_talk.text_ko: 가까이 있어. 림은 밤이 되면 더 나빠져.
elia_talk.burned_text: You seem... distant. Like you can't quite smell the rain anymore.
elia_talk.burned_text_ko: 너... 멀어진 것 같아. 더는 비 냄새를 제대로 맡지 못하는 사람처럼.

elia_talk.speaker: Elia
elia_talk.text: Do you? You burned a Grade 3 back there. How much of 'knowing' is left?
elia_talk.text_ko: 정말 알아? 방금 3등급을 태웠잖아. 네가 안다고 말하는 것 중 얼마나 남아 있어?
elia_talk.burned_text: You used to talk about your training days. Now it's like those stories never happened.
elia_talk.burned_text_ko: 예전엔 훈련하던 날들 이야기를 했었어. 이제는 그런 이야기가 아예 없었던 것 같아.

elia_talk.text: He didn't answer. She was right. She usually was.
elia_talk.text_ko: 그는 대답하지 않았다. 그녀가 맞았다. 대개 그녀가 맞았다.

hidden_stump.text: A tree stump, old enough that its rings had turned to stone.
Someone had carved letters into it once. The rain had eaten most of them, but two remained: A. E.
His hand moved toward the carving before he could stop it.
But his fingers traced the grooves one more time before he pulled away.

sq_echoes_ash_start.text: A figure stood at the edge of the clearing, flickering like a candle in wind.

sq_echoes_ash_start.speaker: Ashen Figure
sq_echoes_ash_start.text: You... burn, don't you? I can smell it. The smoke of what you've lost.

sq_echoes_ash_start.speaker: Ashen Figure
sq_echoes_ash_start.text: I was like you once. But the fragments of what I was are scattered in this forest.

sq_echoes_ash_start.speaker: Ashen Figure
sq_echoes_ash_start.text: Two pieces. Find them. Bring them back. Maybe then I can remember my own name.
The figure's edges blurred. It was already fading.

sq_echoes_ash_frag.text: A faint glow pulses from the ground. A crystallized memory fragment, warm to the touch.
It hums with someone else's past — a child's voice, counting.

elia_forest_walk.text: They walked in silence for a while. The forest watched them with hollow eyes — knotholes in dead trees that looked too round, too deep.

elia_forest_walk.speaker: Elia
elia_forest_walk.text: Touching your temple. You always do that after you burn.
He hadn't noticed. His fingers were pressed against the right side of his skull, tracing circles.

elia_forest_walk.speaker: Elia
elia_forest_walk.text: Habits are the last things to go. Did you know that? A man in Verdan burned everything down to Grade 1. Forgot his own children. But every morning he'd set two extra places at the table.
She said it casually, like weather. But her eyes stayed on his hands.

elia_forest_walk.speaker: Elia
elia_forest_walk.text: That's what you said last time. And the time before that.

elia_forest_walk.speaker: Elia
elia_forest_walk.text: One day 'fine' won't mean anything to you. And you won't even notice.

elia_memory_talk.speaker: Elia
elia_memory_talk.text: What does it feel like? When you burn one.

elia_memory_talk.speaker: Arrel
elia_memory_talk.text: You know how you walk into a room and forget why you came? That. But the room is your entire life, and the door behind you is gone.

elia_memory_talk.speaker: Arrel
elia_memory_talk.text: It doesn't hurt. That's the worst part. If it hurt, you'd know what you lost. But it's just... smooth. Like the gap was always there.

elia_memory_talk.speaker: Arrel
elia_memory_talk.text: Like breathing fire. For about three seconds, you are the most dangerous thing in the world. Then the fire goes out, and you're a little less than you were.
She was quiet for a long time after that.

elia_anchor_talk.speaker: Elia
elia_anchor_talk.text: Arrel. Do you know what I am?

elia_anchor_talk.speaker: Elia
elia_anchor_talk.text: I'm your Anchor. That's what the Bureau calls it. A living reference point — someone whose memories overlap with yours enough that when you burn, the gap doesn't collapse entirely.

elia_anchor_talk.speaker: Arrel
elia_anchor_talk.text: I know what an Anchor is.

elia_anchor_talk.speaker: Elia
elia_anchor_talk.text: Do you? Because three burns ago, you asked me the same question.
He had no way to know if that was true. That was the point.

elia_anchor_talk.speaker: Elia
elia_anchor_talk.text: I'm not trying to scare you. I just need you to understand — if you lose me, if you burn the memories of me, there's no safety net. You fall, and there's no one left who remembers where the ground used to be.

forest_shrine.text: Between the roots of a dead oak, someone had built a shrine.
Stones stacked in careful pyramids. Strips of cloth tied to branches. And in the center, a glass bottle filled with something that caught the dim light like liquid amber.

forest_shrine.speaker: Elia
forest_shrine.text: A memory shrine. People leave them for the things they've burned. As if placing the container near the place it happened might bring the content back.
The amber liquid caught the light once more. Then a flake of ash rain dissolved against the glass, and the glow faded.

dead_burner.text: A body. Or what was left of one.
It sat against a tree, arms at its sides, head tilted back as if watching something in the canopy that had long since moved on.
There were no wounds. No signs of struggle. Just a person who had stopped.

dead_burner.speaker: Elia
dead_burner.text: A burner. Grade 1 burnout. They burned everything — every memory, every sense, every reason to keep breathing. And then they just... sat down.
The body's eyes were open. Clear. Empty. Like two windows in a house where no one lived.

dead_burner.speaker: Elia
dead_burner.text: This is what happens when there's nothing left to burn. Remember that.
He meant it. He hoped he'd still mean it tomorrow.

sq_echoes_ash_complete.speaker: Ashen Figure
sq_echoes_ash_complete.text: You found them. Both of them.
The figure's outline solidified, just for a moment. You saw a face — young, tired, familiar in a way you couldn't place.

sq_echoes_ash_complete.speaker: Ashen Figure
sq_echoes_ash_complete.text: I remember now. I was... counting. Always counting. Take this — it's mine, but I don't need it where I'm going.
The figure dissolved into ash. But the memory remained, crystallized and warm in your hand.
